provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Atomic Energy (Fees and charges) Regulations, 2022.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all activities, practices and services offered by the Commission .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Atomic Energy Act; [Cap. 188] “ category 1 ” means all extremely dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected would cause serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within few minutes; “ category 2 ” means dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected could possibly be serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within a period of several hours or a day; “ category 3 ” means dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected could possibly cause serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within a period of several days; “ Category 4 ” means sources which are unlikely to be dangerous and it is very unlikely that anyone would be permanently injured by these sources; “ category 5 ” means sources which are most unlikely to be dangerous and no one could be permanently injured by these sources and if dispersed, could not permanently injure people; “ Commission ” means the Tanzania Atomic Energy Commission established under section 5 of the Act ; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for atomic energy matters; “ pre-authorization ” means inspection taking action before authorization for new practice; “ prospecting ” means all processes or activities including exploration undertaken for the purpose of determining or discovering whether or not there is deposits of radioactive ore in a given site or area; “ shipment ” means an amount of a particular kind of cargo that is transported to another country or within the country on a ship, train, aeroplane, or other vehicle at one time.

provisions.title: License and registration fee for various practices
provisions.content: The Commission shall charge fees in the manner and modalities prescribed in the First Schedule in respect of every license granted under the Act and any Regulations made there under.

provisions.title: Fees for pre authorization and follow up inspections
provisions.content: (1) The Commission shall charge pre-authorization and follow up inspection fees in the manner and modalities prescribed in the Second Schedule in respect of every inspection activity described under the Act and any regulations made thereunder. (2) The Commission shall charge fees for manufacturers and distributors of food chain materials within the country in the manner and modalities prescribed in the Fourth Schedule.

provisions.title: Fees and charges for activities relating to minerals
provisions.content: A person who intends to undertake any authorised activity relating to prospecting , exploration, mining and processing of radioactive minerals or uranium shall, before undertaking such an activity, pay fees to the Commission in the manner prescribed in the Third Schedule.

provisions.title: Fees for radioactivity analysis of food chain
provisions.content: The Commission shall charge fees for any analysis of radioactivity on food chain and related commodities in the manner prescribed in the Fourth Schedule.

provisions.title: Fees for various services
provisions.content: The Commission may, through an agreement, provide services related to its authorised functions and such services may be chargeable in the manner provided for in the Fifth Schedule.

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The Atomic Energy (Fees and Charges) Regulations, 2011 are hereby revoked. [ GN No. 366 of 2011 ] First Schedule (Made under regulation 4) Fees for licensing/registration of various practices 1. Applications for Authorization of Medical and Non-Medical Radiation Generating Devices and Radiation Sources, research clearance, scrap metals S/No. Type of service Charges (Tshs) 1 For authorization to use, possess, operate, hire, import, export or transfer of medical diagnostic equipment’s 200,000/= per unit. 2 For authorization to use and possess, operate, hire, Import or export CT scanner, therapeutic (teletherapy and brachytherapy), angio-suite, MRI, Nuclear Medicine, PET CT and biological irradiation facilities 300,000/= per unit 3 For authorization to use, possess, operate, hire, import, export or transfer non-medical radiation emitting equipment, device, communication base stations, premises for storage of radiation sources, and research clearance 300,000/= per unit, base station or research 4 For authorization to use, possess, operate, import, export, hire, au transfer class 4 laser product, cyclotron, Non-Medical Linear Accelerator, Fixed X-ray or Radioactive Devices for Non Destructive Testing, Cargo or Container Inspection scanner, or other devices of considerable significant high risk, soil sample containing radioactive materials, Natural Occurring Radioactive Materials (NORMs); and screening of consignment of scrap metals. 500,000/= per unit/consignment 5 For authorization to use, possess, operate, hire, transfer, export or import industrial irradiation source/facility, nuclear research reactor, radioactive minerals, 5,000,000/= per unit 6 For authorization of siting, construction, operation or decommissioning of Nuclear Power Plant Tanzania Shillings 10,000,000/= 7 Additional fee for authorization to import all Ionizing and Non-Ionizing Radiation emitting equipment or devices of age above 8 years since manufactured. Tanzania Shilling 1,000,000/= per unit 2. Registration of Qualified personnel, Technical Service Providers and a License to Transport Radioactive Sources S/No. Type of service Charges (Tshs) 1 For the registration of qualified personnel to administer ionizing radiation to persons. Free of charge 2 For the registration of qualified personnel to operate, install, repair and maintenance of radiation devices or apparatus or plant. 50,000/= for every five (5) years 3 For Authorization to transport Category 1 to 5 radioactive material, Minerals containing radioactive materials and Natural Occurring Radioactive Materials (NORMs) within and or on transit through Tanzania (excluding reimbursable) 1,000,000/= per service excluding reimbursable. 4 For Authorization to provision of technical services such as Personnel dosimetry, workplace monitoring, standards calibration, environmental monitoring, radio analytical measurements, repair and maintenance of nuclear equipment, supplier of radiation devices, transporters of radioactive materials, storage of radiation devices, radiation protection training services providers, quality assurance and quality control services providers 1,000,000/= per service excluding reimbursable. Second Schedule (Made under regulation 5) Pre-authorisation and follow up inspection fees Applications for Non-routine inspection fee for radiation facility or activity for Medical and Non-Medical shall be charged as follows:— S/No. Type of practice Fees (Tshs) 1. For assessment and inspection of Radiology X-ray unit 100,000/= per unit excluding reimbursable. 2. For assessment and inspection of Radiation emitting equipment, device and communication base stations, and radioactive material storage facility and other low and moderate risk practices 200,000/= per unit or center excluding reimbursable. 3. For assessment and inspection of class 4 laser product, Linear Accelerator, Fixed X-ray or Radioactive Devices for Non Destructive Testing, Cargo or Container Inspection scanner, Cyclotron or CT scan, Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), scrap metals warehouse and other devices of considerable significant high risk excluding reimbursable 500,000/= per unit excluding reimbursable. 4. For each assessment or inspection during siting, construction, commissioning, operation and decommissioning of mines containing naturally occurring radioactive minerals or conventional mines, assessment of radon gas concentration in underground mines, irradiator facility 5,000,000/= per service excluding reimbursable. 5. For each assessment and inspection during siting, construction, commissioning, operation and decommissioning of Nuclear Power, Nuclear Research Reactor, Radioactive minerals mine, processing, disposal and tailings facility 10,000,000/= per service excluding reimbursable Third Schedule (Made under regulation 6) Fees and charges for activities relating to prospecting, exploration, mining and processing of radioactive minerals S/No. Type of practice Charges (Tshs) 1. Permit for prospecting of radioactive minerals 20,000,000/= per mining 2. Permit for Siting and construction of a Mine and or Processing Facility 15,000,0000/= per mining 3. Permit for Mining or Processing of radioactive minerals 20,000,000/= per Mining License 4. Permit for transport and on-transit concentrated radioactive ores 7,000,000/= per shipment 5. Permit for Decommission and close mine or ore processing facility 20,000,0000/= per mine/facility 6. Verification in respect of decommissioning processing of radioactive minerals mine and its processing facilities 20,000,000/= per annum per mine or processing facility Fourth Schedule (Made under regulations 5(2) and 7) Fees for radioactivity analysis of food chain and related commodities Category A: For all Imports of Food Chain Materials, Tobacco and Tobacco Products shall be charged as follows: 1. Free of Charge for fertilizers. 2. Free of charge for consignment whole Freight on board value does not exceed one million Tanzanian shillings. 3. 0.4 percent of the Freight on Board value for all import consignments above Tanzanian Shilling 1,000,000 up to an equivalent of one billion Tanzanian shillings. 4. Tanzanian shillings four million for the consignment whole Freight on Board value is above to an equivalent of Tanzanian shillings one billion. Category B: For all exports of Food Chain Materials including fertilizers, tobacco and tobacco products and imported relief foods 1. Free of Charges for consignment whose Freight on board value does not exceed two thousand US dollars or its equivalent to Tanzanian Shillings 2. 0.1 percent of the Freight on Board value for all consignments with Freight on Board value above two thousand US dollars (or its equivalent to Tanzanian Shillings) up to one billion Tanzanian shillings. 3. Tanzanian shillings two million for the consignment whose Freight on Board value is above Tanzanian shillings one billion. Category C: Extra Copy of Radioactivity Analysis Certificate Each extra certified copy of the Radioactivity Analysis Certificate issued under category A and B of this Schedule for the same consignment shall be charged Tanzanian shillings ten thousand. Category D: For all manufacturers, distributors, processors and milling of food chain materials, tobacco and tobacco products and licensees of underground and water reservoir activities i. Radioactivity analysis fees for food chain materials, tobacco and tobacco products for all manufacturers, distributors, processors and milling shall be charged per annum as follows: 1. Free for Micro Enterprises and Small Enterprises (SMEs). 2. Tanzania shillings two hundred and fifty thousand for Medium Enterprises (MSEs). 3. Tanzania shillings three hundred thousand (300,000/=) for Large Enterprises (LE) Note: According to the Small and Medium Enterprise Development Policy (2003), Tanzania defines categories of enterprises as follows: a) Micro Enterprises as up to 4 employees or up to 5 Million investments in Machinery b) Small enterprises are mostly formalized undertakings engaging between 5 and 49 employees or with capital investment from Tshs. 5 million to Tshs.200 million. c) Medium enterprises employ between 50 and 99 people or use capital investment from Tshs. 200 million to TZS 800 million. d) Large enterprises employ at least 100 people or have machinery investment above TZS 800 million. ii. For radioactivity analysis license to possess and use underground and water reservoir a) Tanzania shillings 50,000/= for radioactivity analysis license to use and possess underground and reservoir waters in every three years. Fifth Schedule (Made under regulations 8) Fees and charges for other related activities or services S/No. Type of practice Charges 1. To Provide Structural Design and layout of diagnostic X-ray facilities, Review of radiation shielding radiological facilities, perform quality Tanzania Shillings 200,000/= per unit excluding reimbursable control tests of diagnostic radiation equipment and testing of integrity of package of radioactive sources for transport and maintenance of survey meters. 2. Measurement of a sample by either gamma spectrometry, Energy Dispersive X-Ray Fluorescence (EDXRF) or Total Reflection X-Ray Fluorescence (TXRF) Tanzania Shillings 100,000/ and 25% discount for a student 3. Personal dosimetry services for personal dosimetry services per Themoluminecense Dosimeters (TLDs). Tanzania Shillings 100,000 per TLD For lost Themoluminecense Dosimeters (TLDs) Charged as per market price of the Themoluminecense Dosimeters 4. Calibration of radiation Survey meter, Electronic personnel dosimeter, individual and environmental monitoring detector Tanzania Shillings 150,000/= for resident excluding reimbursable USD 150 per unit for non-resident excluding reimbursable 5. Consultation services on the proper use of X-ray machines. Tanzania Shillings 500,000 /= excluding reimbursable 6. Calibration, repair and maintenance of Moisture and density gauges and other radiation emitting equipment Tanzania Shillings 1,900,000/ per unit excluding reimbursable 7. Installation, repair and maintenance of X -ray machine Tanzania Shillings 2,500,000/= per each machine excluding reimbursable 8. For Transport, Storage and conditioning of a radioactive waste at Central Radioactive Waste Management Facility (CRWMF) Tanzania shillings 4,000,000/= per source excluding reimbursable History of this document 01 July 2022 this version Assented to Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 25 Commenced Related documents Revokes Atomic Energy (Fees and Charges) Regulations, 2011 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all. definitions.definition: all extremely dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected would cause serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within few minutes

dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected could possibly be serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within a period of several hours or a day
dangerous radioactive material which if not safely handled, managed or securely protected could possibly cause serious injury to persons handling or in contact with it within a period of several days
sources which are unlikely to be dangerous and it is very unlikely that anyone would be permanently injured by these sources
sources which are most unlikely to be dangerous and no one could be permanently injured by these sources and if dispersed, could not permanently injure people
the Tanzania Atomic Energy Commission established under section 5 of the Act
the Minister responsible for atomic energy matters
inspection taking action before authorization for new practice
all processes or activities including exploration undertaken for the purpose of determining or discovering whether or not there is deposits of radioactive ore in a given site or area
an amount of a particular kind of cargo that is transported to another country or within the country on a ship, train, aeroplane, or other vehicle at one time.
